question: Define a base class `MediaItem` with a virtual function `play()`. Derive `AudioFile` and `VideoFile` classes from `MediaItem`. Override `play()` in each derived class to simulate playing an audio or video file (e.g., printing 'Playing audio: [title]' or 'Playing video: [filename]'). Use an array of `MediaItem` pointers to demonstrate playing various media types polymorphically.

test_cases.expected:
--- Playing Media Collection ---
Playing audio: My Favorite Song
Playing video: vacation_2023.mp4 (Title: Holiday Memories)
Playing audio: Podcast Episode 1
--------------------------------

errors: Missing virtual destructor in the `MediaItem` base class. This can lead to undefined behavior and resource leaks if derived classes allocate resources and are deleted via a base class pointer.
Memory leak: Dynamically allocated `MediaItem` objects are not deallocated using `delete`, leading to unreleased memory.
